# DarkSlide v1.0.1

DarkSlide 1.0.1 is a focused reliability release. It reworks automatic film-base detection so dense black-and-white and dim or expired color negatives no longer crush to black on import, and it drops the "Beta" product labels now that the app is out of beta.

## Highlights

- Automatic clear-film-base estimation is now region-based and confidence-scored, clustering base candidates and gating them with a texture check, with a bright-percentile refusal fallback when no reliable region is found.
- A decode-time crush guard demotes catastrophic base estimates to a conservative reference, and density-balance clamp hits are now rejected as untrustworthy instead of being trusted at face value.
- A fallback ladder chooses the best available base source in order: manual > roll > frame rebate > border sample > conservative default.
- Black-and-white imports use a luminance-first Dmin when the base is estimated, and previews start with a neutral white balance when the estimate is low-confidence, instead of silently guessing.
- A one-time low-confidence notice surfaces uncertain estimates to the user, backed by a full film-base decision record in the debug readout for troubleshooting.
- The confidence-aware estimate is threaded through both the CPU and GPU (WebGPU) render paths, and through the 16-bit RAW analysis path, so preview and export stay consistent regardless of which pipeline handles a given image.
- Dropped the "Beta" product labels from the UI now that DarkSlide has shipped a stable 1.0 release.

## Why This Matters

Dense B&W negatives and dim or expired color stock previously confused the automatic base picker often enough to blow out the estimate and collapse the converted image to black. The new estimator is deliberately conservative: when it isn't confident, it falls back to a safer reference and tells the user, rather than producing a bad conversion silently.

## Desktop Downloads

GitHub Actions builds and attaches installers for each supported desktop platform:

- macOS: universal Apple Silicon and Intel `.dmg`
- Windows: `.msi` and NSIS `.exe`
- Linux: `.deb` and `.AppImage`

The desktop app adds RAW import, native dialogs, file associations, scanning sessions, external editor handoff, and local update checks. The browser app remains available for JPEG, PNG, TIFF, and WebP workflows without installing anything.

## Known Notes

- macOS builds are not notarized. On first launch, macOS may require opening the app through System Settings > Privacy & Security > Open Anyway.
- Windows and Linux builds are unsigned.
- RAW support is available in the desktop app and remains an advanced workflow; JPEG, PNG, TIFF, and WebP are the stable browser-supported formats.

## Checks

This release is built by GitHub Actions on macOS, Windows, and Linux. The release workflow also keeps the generated installers as workflow artifacts so they can be inspected even if a release upload has to be retried.
